task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: How to request a license via the web

abstract.p: The licenses of the printer are stored on a Wibu dongle. This USB key is situated in the Windows controller (also called printer license key).
It is similar as the SAM key that is used to access the SDS and HTML TSM. This procedure describes how to request a license via the web.

section.title: Additional useful information
section.p: You only need to retrieve a Dongle summary file (<dongle ID>-SummaryFile.zip) from the printer to create a request.
You can get this file via the Settings Editor. This zip file includes all the information required to create a license file:

li.p: A <dongle ID>.WibuCmRac file (WiBu dongle context file)
A <dongle ID>.xml file (including the printer hardware identifier value and the printer serial number).
The installation wizard must have completed (except license installation)
System Administrator password is required.
A WiBu dongle must be installed on the Windows Controller. Check this if anything goes wrong.
A valid license number for this printer is needed (provided by the back-office).
A valid order number is needed (provided by the back-office).

note.p: If the controller is replaced by a new one, you need a new license file.
This is because the license file is partly based on the 'Hardware identifier' which is specific for each controller motherboard.
The SAM key should never be in the printer when you retrieve the dongle context file!

li.p: Verify the (service) SAM key is not in the printer.
Start the printer.
Open the Settings Editor Access the settings editor via any web browser, by typing in the printer's:
IP address, or
Hostname
Browse to: Support > Licenses
Click " Create summary file" of license dongle:
The System Administrator login window displays (only if not already logged in) :
Enter the System Administrator password.
Click Log in.
The Create and download summary file of license dongle window displays:
Click Create summary file of license dongle.
A <dongle ID>-SummaryFile.zip file is created:
Click the <dongle ID>-SummaryFile.zip file name.
The following window displays at the bottom of the screen:
Select Save > Save as.
The Save as window displays.
Browse to a directory on the workstation and click Save. The Create and download the dongle summary file window displays.
Click OK. The Licenses tab displays.
Goto the web page http://activation.cpp.canon
Fill out the requested fields. Make sure the email address you enter is valid. In case the email is incorrect you need to contact your local supplier.
The License number and Order number should have been provided by your local supplier.
Enter also the code that is shown in the image in your browser.
Click the next button to continue.
In case the order number or the license number is incorrect an error is reported.
The following page is shown:
Select the Upload summaryfile button and select the zip file that you created earlier.
Within a few minutes a mail is sent to the given email address.
It contains a link to the generated license file.
Install the license: see Install a license received via e-mail
